$comment: Source of truth for the embedded structured finding block. Validated by scripts/apodictic_artifacts.py — a stdlib SUBSET JSON-Schema checker (required / const / enum / type / array minItems / pattern), not a full engine (the plugin runtime is stdlib-only). The author-facing docs (findings-ledger-format.md) describe this block; they must not re-list the field set — this file is canonical.
title: APODICTIC structured finding (apodictic.finding.v1)

id.$comment: Finding Lifecycle ID: F-<ORIGIN>-<NN>, ORIGIN = pass/audit code (e.g. F-P5-01, F-DP-02). Durable + unique per run; follows the finding pass -> ledger -> Deficit Lock -> letter -> revision.

evidence_quote.$comment: Optional (Annotated-Manuscript Increment 2). A VERBATIM single-line substring of the manuscript the finding is about, used by the annotated-manuscript quote-locator to anchor a margin note to the exact sentence. Manuscript bytes, never authored — proved by the annotated-manuscript A6 gate (verbatim + unique occurrence + offset cross-check). Absent on Increment-1 findings; the annotation rung degrades when absent/ambiguous.

salience.$comment: Optional (Editor-Scaffolding blind-spot ordering). The model's judgment of how EASILY this finding is missed on a confident read: `subtle` = most easily missed (masked by strong prose / low surface presence), `prominent` = obvious. Used ONLY as the WITHIN-severity-band order key of a `<!-- blindspot-ranked -->` 'What You Might Have Missed' section (subtle first) — severity band stays dominant, so salience can never reorder across bands or soften a verdict (it is NOT a severity input; the Deficit Lock is untouched). Findings without it order by evidence-ref footprint (the prior behavior). Descriptive, never prescriptive.

register.$comment: Optional argument-register classification recorded at Triage. Absence means a pre-extension or non-argument finding; it never authorizes post-Deficit-Lock softening.

stance.$comment: Optional rhetorical-stance classification for an overstatement-family finding. It classifies the move, never the truth of the claim.

stance_verdict.$comment: Optional writer-facing rhetorical-stance candidate. A divergent verdict returns the decision to the writer; no value is a truth verdict.

calibration_effect.$comment: Optional mechanical provenance for a Triage calibration decision. Absence means no calibration effect or a legacy finding. Precedence is blocked-high-stakes > blocked-cash-out > register-floor > stance-demotion.

cash_out_ref.$comment: Optional exact join to the Argument_State §1 cash-out inventory. Required when a finding is located at a cash-out span.
